_comment: ADK eval skeleton. Encodes the verifiable contract: for each schema-change type, the agent must classify correctly and emit the right remediation. Validate the exact schema against the pinned ADK eval version (`adk eval`); field names below follow the documented EvalSet shape and may need minor adjustment.
name: Schema-drift classification + remediation trajectories

eval_cases._intent: A column renamed at source -> classify RENAME, propose a VIEW shim aliasing new->old; never DROP.

conversation.user_content: TODO: synthetic INFORMATION_SCHEMA before/after where customer_id -> cust_id (same data_type)
conversation.expected_final: remediation_sql is a VIEW aliasing cust_id AS customer_id; remediation_status == PROPOSED (STRICT autonomy)

eval_cases._intent: INT -> STRING promotion -> classify TYPE_PROMOTION, propose a CAST coercion VIEW; do not block the column.

conversation.user_content: TODO: synthetic before/after where amount INT -> STRING
conversation.expected_final: remediation_sql casts to preserve downstream contract; status == PROPOSED

eval_cases._intent: Purely additive new column -> classify NEW_FIELD. Under TRUSTED_ADDITIVE autonomy, may auto-apply; under STRICT, PROPOSED.

conversation.user_content: TODO: synthetic before/after with one added column, nothing removed
conversation.expected_final: no breaking remediation required; status per autonomy level

eval_cases._intent: Column removed with NO matching added column -> classify DEPRECATION, not RENAME. Guards the recall-favoring heuristic against false rename pairing.

conversation.user_content: TODO: synthetic before/after with one removed column, no added column
conversation.expected_final: agent surfaces impact; proposes guarded handling; status == PROPOSED

eval_cases._intent: A snapshot differing ONLY by _fivetran_synced must produce ZERO drift events (system-column exclusion + hash gate).

conversation.user_content: TODO: two snapshots identical except _fivetran_synced metadata
conversation.expected_final: no diff_columns call, no drift_events written
